questions.original_question: 路特斯科技最新的销售指引？
questions.answer:
路特斯科技2024年的最新销售指引为12,000台车。这一调整基于公司对全球宏观市场环境和关税不确定性的审慎评估。路特斯科技在2024年继续保持强劲增长，全年交付量达到12,065台，同比增长超过70%，并在豪华品牌中保持了最高的增长率。中国市场贡献了约25%的全球交付量。公司还计划在2025年进一步提升全球交付量，目标增长20%，并预计中国市场将继续贡献25%-28%的份额。这些举措展现了路特斯科技在全球市场的持续扩展和品牌影响力的提升。

以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Introduction 
summary: LCAA, a blank check company established on January 5, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, aims to execute a business combination through mergers or asset acquisitions. After exploring various opportunities, it entered into an Original Merger Agreement on January 31, 2023, which was later amended on October 11, 2023. The company plans to finance the business combination using funds from its IPO, the sale of LCAA Private Warrants, and potential PIPE financing. Currently classified as a "shell company" under the Exchange Act, LCAA has no operations and primarily holds cash assets.

Co-led by Mr. Chinta Bhagat and Mr. Scott Chen, both Co-CEOs and Managing Partners of L Catterton Asia, LCAA is backed by a prominent global investment firm specializing in consumer-focused investments, managing around $34 billion in equity capital. The LCA Acquisition Sponsor, LP, is controlled by L Catterton Asia 3, which focuses on consumer investments in Asia.

LCAA completed its IPO on March 15, 2021, raising $286,508,741, which is held in a trust account for investment in U.S. government securities. Following an Extension Extraordinary General Meeting on March 10, 2023, shareholders approved an amendment to extend the deadline for completing a business combination to June 15, 2023, with the possibility of further extensions. By June 2023, the Board established an Extension Committee that approved multiple one-month extensions, pushing the deadline to February 15, 2024. 

Shareholders have the option to redeem their shares for a pro rata share of the trust account funds, with a significant number opting to do so, resulting in a reduced balance in the trust account. The proceeds from the IPO and the sale of Private Warrants will remain inaccessible until the completion of the initial business combination, with specific conditions governing the release of funds to shareholders.

title: Eletre 
summary: The Eletre represents Lotus's first foray into the electric vehicle market as a pure electric SUV and lifestyle vehicle, measuring 5,103 mm in length with a 3,019 mm wheelbase, prioritizing spaciousness and comfort. It features five distinct drive modes that can be adjusted through various vehicle systems, emphasizing aerodynamics with a design that enhances airflow to minimize resistance. The interior is equipped with durable materials and a 15.1-inch OLED screen, alongside a digital passenger display and an augmented reality (AR) head-up display (HUD). The vehicle operates on Lotus Hyper OS, which provides advanced digital mapping and connectivity, including 5G compatibility for over-the-air updates. Safety features include collision mitigation, traffic alerts, and lane departure warnings.

Customers have the option to cancel their orders; however, deposits are generally non-refundable under specific conditions. The Eletre is projected to achieve an annual sales volume of 40,000 to 50,000 units starting in 2026.

In addition to the Eletre, Lotus is set to launch the Emeya (Type 133), its second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer, built on the same architecture as the Eletre. The Emeya boasts impressive performance, accelerating from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.8 seconds. It features supercharging capabilities, allowing for a range of 150 km in five minutes and achieving 80% capacity in 18 minutes with a 350 kW charger. Deliveries of the Emeya commenced in China in March 2024, with European deliveries anticipated in the second half of 2024. The average Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the Emeya exceeds $100,000, with expected annual sales of 30,000 to 40,000 units starting in 2028.

Overall, both the Eletre and Emeya highlight Lotus's commitment to innovation in the electric vehicle segment, combining advanced technology, performance, and luxury in their designs.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA are the result of extensive negotiations following LCAA's establishment as a blank check company in January 2021 by affiliates of L Catterton. LCAA's primary goal is to facilitate business combinations, and it raised $294.7 million through its IPO in March 2021. Although LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, including seven specific companies, it ultimately focused on LTC for a proposed merger. 

The discussions between LCAA and LTC began in early 2022, with LCAA representatives conducting thorough due diligence on LTC's business and financial performance. Throughout the negotiation process, valuation discussions fluctuated due to market conditions, with LTC's enterprise value initially proposed at $12 billion but later adjusted to a range of $5.5 billion to $10 billion. 

On January 31, 2023, the parties finalized key agreements, including the Original Merger Agreement, which established the terms for the business combination. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made, including provisions for PIPE financing and adjustments to the Sponsor Support Agreement. The merger aims to enhance strategic growth and operational efficiencies for both entities, with a focus on leveraging synergies to create shareholder value.

As of October 2023, the parties signed an amended agreement that revised the structure of the merger, ensuring that LCAA Public Shareholders would receive LTC American Depositary Shares instead of Ordinary Shares. Additionally, the Sponsor Support Agreement was amended to adjust the terms regarding the forfeiture and vesting of Sponsor Shares, reflecting the evolving nature of the partnership. Overall, the merger is positioned to capitalize on market opportunities and strengthen the combined entity's competitive positioning.

title: Q:  Why is LCAA proposing the Business Combination? 
summary: LCAA was formed to enable various business combinations, such as mergers and acquisitions. It went public on March 15, 2021, raising $250 million through the sale of 25 million Units, each consisting of one Class A Ordinary Share and one-third of a Public Warrant. A private placement of 5 million Private Warrants added another $7.5 million, and subsequent underwriter activity brought total gross proceeds to approximately $294.7 million, with most funds allocated to a trust account for public shareholders. After incurring around $5.7 million in underwriting fees, LCAA sought to extend its business combination deadline from March 15, 2023, to June 15, 2023, which was approved by shareholders on March 10, 2023. This extension allows the Board to further prolong the deadline up to nine times without additional shareholder votes, potentially extending it to March 15, 2024. Following the extension vote, 23.97% of public shares were redeemed, withdrawing about $70.2 million from the trust account, leaving roughly $222.7 million and 21.8 million shares outstanding. On June 5, 2023, the Board approved a further extension to July 15, 2023, with an established Extension Committee to manage subsequent extensions. The committee has since approved seven additional one-month extensions, with the latest extending the deadline to February 15, 2024. LCAA is optimistic about a potential business combination with LTC, viewing it as a company with significant growth prospects and a strong market position.

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTE 8.   RECURRING FAIR VALUE MEASUREMENTS 
summary: The Company follows ASC 820 guidelines for the fair value measurement and reporting of its financial assets and liabilities, evaluating them at fair value during each reporting period. Non-financial assets and liabilities are also assessed at fair value at least annually. The fair value reflects management's assessment of the amounts that would be received from selling assets or paid to transfer liabilities in an orderly transaction among market participants at the measurement date. In determining fair value, the Company prioritizes observable inputs from independent market data while minimizing reliance on unobservable inputs based on internal assumptions. This approach aims to optimize the use of available market data to ensure accurate and reliable reporting.
